name: The Adept
effect: The next ten natural 1's you roll become 20's instead

name: The Gifted
effect: Instantly gain a level of a random class, other than your own

name: The Champion
effect: Increase your two highest ability scores by 2 each (max 20).

name: The Saint
effect: You only need to succeed one death saving throw to stabilize

name: The Angel
effect: You gain a divine ally who follows your every command. Their challenge rating is equal to half your level (rounding down). When they reach 0HP they vanish.

name: The Merchant
effect: You gain 1,000 gold x Character Level

name: The Dopplegänger
effect: Roll on the reincarnate table. You gain one trait of choice from that race (not including attributes)

name: The Blacksmith
effect: Gain a weapon one rarity better than your current weapon, of the same type (DM's choice)

name: The Mentor
effect: Gain expertise in a skill you're proficient in or proficiency in a skill you're not proficient in.

name: The Elemental
effect: Gain resistance to a random element

name: The Wizard
effect: Choose any spell equal to half your level (round down). You may cast that spell once per long rest. If the spell requires a save it is DC 13

name: The Savage
effect: Your critical hits now do triple damage instead of double

name: The Hare
effect: Gain +10 to your movement speed

name: The Enlightened
effect: You are given the power to ask the GM one single question at a time of your choosing. They must answer truthfully and as helpful as possible.

name: The Satiated
effect: You only need to drink half a potion to gain its effects

name: The Crusader
effect: Roll for a random creature type. Your attack rolls always have advantage against that type

name: The Ally
effect: Choose another player, you can transfer life from them or to them as a bonus action as long as they are willing

name: The Hero
effect: The next time all of the other members of your party are at 0HP, you regain your full HP and remove any negative status effects, and regain 1 slot for each spell level you can cast (if any)

name: The Martyr
effect: At the moment of your death, you may cast the wish spell. This can be anything the wish spell is capable of. If you do this, no magic including this or any other wish can bring you back from the dead

name: The Charitable
effect: Choose another player and draw two Blessed cards from the deck. They must choose one for themselves and give you the other.

name: The Lummox
effect: The next ten natural 20's you roll become 1's instead

name: The Ambivalent
effect: The next time you level up, you must choose to progress in a level of a random class instead of your current class

name: The Downtrodden
effect: Drop your lowest ability score by 4.

name: The Reaper
effect: The next time you roll a 1 on a death saving throw, you disintegrate

name: The Devil
effect: You gain a demonic stalker with a challenge rating equal to your Character Level - 2. They will wait to strike when you are the most vulnerable

name: The Pauper
effect: You incur a debt of 1,000 gold x your Character Level with a powerful Lord. If you don't pay them back in one month's time, there will be dire consequences.

name: The Reborn
effect: Roll on the reincarnation table and switch genders. Your current race is replaced by the new one along with all of your racial traits and attribute bonuses

name: The Thief
effect: Your best item loses all of its magic and features

name: The Commoner
effect: Lose proficiency in a random skill you're proficient in

name: The Cursed
effect: Gain vulnerability to a random element

name: The Thrall
effect: Choose CON, WIS, or DEX. You gain disadvantage on any magic spell that requires a save from that attribute

name: The Frail
effect: Any critical hit against you will do triple damage instead of double

name: The Tortoise
effect: Lose -10 to your movement speed

name: The Trickster
effect: The GM will lie to you at some point on a critical piece of information, regardless of your roll. Once the party has acted on the information, the GM should reveal the lie has been used.

name: The Desiccated
effect: Potions no longer work on you

name: The Nightmare
effect: Roll for a random creature type. That creature type always attacks with advantage against you

name: The Leech
effect: Choose another player. Any damage they receive that would bring their HP below 0 is siphoned from yours to keep them at 1 HP, if possible.

name: The Rat
effect: The next time all the other members of your party are at 0HP you are transported to the last safe place you rested that's far from any monsters or danger.

name: The Vengeful
effect: Upon your death you deal 10d10 force damage to every creature within 60ft.

name: The Backstabber
effect: Draw two Accursed cards. Keep one and bestow the other on a player of your choice.
